behaviors.description: The speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels so that the summary can attribute statements to specific attendees.
behaviors.justification: Escalation evidence: "speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels" and "so that the summary can attribute statements." This depends on real STT model diarization/output quality; a stubbed transcript would hide speaker-label failures. Rejected higher level Workflow because the behavior text only requires correctness of this single model-output property, not multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run a real audio sample with multiple speakers through the actual STT API and assert transcript segments include correct speaker labels that can be mapped to attendee identities for downstream summarization.

behaviors.description: Transcript parsing extracts explicit task assignments (assignee + task text) into the summary input structure.
behaviors.justification: No escalation phrase requires real infra or model output; behavior is parser/structure logic: "Transcript parsing extracts explicit task assignments ... into the summary input structure." Failure boundary is local extraction logic.
behaviors.test_description: Call the parsing function with fixture transcript text containing explicit assignments and assert produced structures contain correct assignee and task text pairs.

behaviors.description: The live summary output contains at least one action item for every attendee who was assigned a task during the meeting.
behaviors.justification: Escalation evidence: "live summary output" and coverage condition "for every attendee who was assigned a task." Correctness depends on what the real summarizing LLM includes/omits; deterministic mocks can always satisfy this and hide omission failures. Rejected Workflow because behavior does not explicitly require validating multiple agentic handoffs, only final summary completeness.
behaviors.test_description: Provide a transcript fixture with explicit assignments for multiple attendees, call the real summarization model, and assert each assigned attendee appears in at least one generated action item.

behaviors.description: Decision extraction logic routes identified decisions into the `decisions` section schema rather than generic narrative fields.
behaviors.justification: No phrase requires real OS/network/subprocess or real model judgment; this is deterministic routing behavior: "logic routes identified decisions into the `decisions` section schema."
behaviors.test_description: Invoke the decision-routing function with pre-identified decision items and narrative items, then assert decisions are placed only in the `decisions` schema field and excluded from narrative fields.

behaviors.description: Key decisions mentioned in the transcript appear in the live summary's `decisions` section, not buried in the narrative.
behaviors.justification: Escalation evidence: "appear in the live summary's `decisions` section, not buried in the narrative." This checks LLM output content/placement quality; mocked summaries hide real model failures to surface decisions correctly. Rejected Workflow because the behavior text targets one output-quality property, not a multi-agentic pipeline sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Send transcripts containing clear decision statements to the real summarization model and assert those decisions are present in the generated `decisions` section and absent from narrative-only placement.

behaviors.description: When the audio file is longer than 60 minutes, the service splits it into segments and processes them sequentially, merging the results into a single coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: Escalation evidence: "splits it into segments and processes them sequentially, merging the results into a single coherent summary." This is explicitly multi-step orchestration across repeated model calls plus merge coherence, so single-step Agentic is too low.
behaviors.test_description: Use a real >60-minute recording, run full segmented processing with actual STT/summary calls per chunk, and verify chunk order handling, complete coverage, and coherent merged final summary structure.

behaviors.description: The full pipeline (upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output) completes without manual intervention for a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: Escalation evidence: "full pipeline (upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output)" and "for a real meeting recording." This requires end-to-end execution with multiple agentic stages in sequence plus real infrastructure interaction; lower levels cannot expose full pipeline breakages.
behaviors.test_description: Run an end-to-end test with a real meeting audio file through production-like upload, transcription, summarization, and output delivery paths; assert successful completion and valid structured payload without human intervention.
